---
title: "UVM Economics AI Bootcamp"
subtitle: "April 2026 · Department of Economics, University of Vermont"
toc: true
---

A two-session faculty bootcamp on AI tools for teaching and research, delivered in April 2026 at the University of Vermont.

---

## Session 1: Getting Started with AI Tools

**April 22, 11:00–12:30, Old Mill A500**

| Deck | Format |
|------|--------|
| Session 1: Agentic AI Bootcamp (Aslim & Beam) | [PDF](Session1_Slides/session_1_slides.pdf) |

### What we covered

- What AI tools exist and the difference between chat-based and agentic tools
- Context windows, standing instructions, and planning — the key concepts
- What we've actually used these for (and what was fun but saved zero time)
- Voice files: making AI output sound like you
- Skills: reusable workflows you can share
- Live demos: website redesign and custom skill creation

---

## Session 2: Research Workflows, Teaching & Applications

**April 27, 11:00–12:30, Old Mill A500**

| Deck | Format |
|------|--------|
| Session 2: Research Workflows, Teaching & Applications (Aslim & Beam) | [PDF](Session2_Slides/session_2_slides.pdf) |

### What we covered

- **Emily: Research workflows** — code auditing a replication package with [`/code-review`](applications.html#code-review), paper auditing with [`/econ-audit`](applications.html#econ-audit) and [`/review-paper`](applications.html#review-paper), running Lee bounds live
- **Erkmen: Teaching & applications** — building a lecture-builder skill with [`/academic-beamer-deck`](applications.html#academic-beamer-deck), stress-testing a research idea with [`/research-brainstorm`](applications.html#research-brainstorm), web scraping with [`/find-data`](applications.html#find-data)

### What to try next

- **Research**: Try the data exploration → analysis plan workflow on your own data
- **Teaching**: Draft a new assignment, create practice problems, or build a study guide
- **Code**: Run `/econ-audit` on a paper before you submit it
- **Writing**: Use Claude Code to revise a draft, check references, or restructure a section

---

## Resources

- [Getting Set Up](prep.qmd) — prep guide for installing and configuring AI tools
- [Applications & Skills](applications.qmd) — the skills and demos shown in the bootcamp
- [Outside Resources](resources.qmd) — broader reading and tools
